title: Midnight Signals — AI Spooky Short for Teens
summary: Develop a 5–7 minute AI-generated spooky short film for a teen audience (PG-13 vibe): suspense over gore, phone-native storytelling, clear consent/safety boundaries, and a fast, reproducible AI production pipeline (script → boards → animatics → final).

tracking.next: Review the free local motion pass for story quality; optionally upgrade later with Hugging Face or another provider if credentials/GPU become available.
done: Video lane switched away from Runway-only blocking to a provider adapter with local no-cost fallback first.
Selected local_motionfx and verified artifacts/animatic_v3_motionfx.mp4 (first_frame_size=1920x1080; ffprobe unavailable, verified first frame with imageio).

control.delivery_target: free local motion animatic with optional provider upgrades
acceptance: Five teens are visibly rendered as characters, not seat markers or abstract geometry.
The boards visibly mirror the supplied camp reference: tent back-center, lantern left foreground, sign on right, central fire, moonlit forest palette.
Lighting reads as warm fire key plus cool moon rim with phones-only darkness where called for.
If the lane can only produce blocking diagrams or placeholder boards, it must report blocked and label the artifact placeholder instead of marking it review-ready.
